sections.content:
Most AI tools offer a free tier and paid plans. Here's what that actually means in practice.

**What free plans give you:**
- Access to a capable model — usually good enough for everyday tasks
- A limited number of messages per day (ChatGPT free: limited GPT-4o messages, then falls back to a basic model)
- Basic file uploads on some tools
- The tool may use your conversations to improve the model

**What paid plans add (typically $20–$25/month):**
- The most powerful model without limits
- Faster responses, especially when the service is busy
- Higher message limits or no limits
- Better privacy — most paid plans don't use your chats for training
- More features (memory, longer documents, voice, image generation)
- Priority access when servers are under load

**ChatGPT Plus (~$20/month):** Full GPT-4o, image generation (DALL-E), memory, voice mode, Custom GPTs, file uploads.

**Claude Pro (~$20/month):** More messages with Opus and Sonnet, Projects (persistent context), longer conversations, priority access.

**Gemini Advanced (~$20/month):** Gemini Ultra model, Google Workspace integration (Gmail, Docs, Drive), longer context.

**Perplexity Pro (~$20/month):** More Pro searches per day, access to different AI models, file uploads.

**When the free plan is enough:** Personal use, occasional tasks, getting started, testing if AI is useful for you.

**When to upgrade:** You're hitting message limits, you need the most capable model for important work, or privacy matters for your use case.

sections.bad: Sign up for every paid AI plan to make sure you always have the best tool available.
sections.good: Start with free plans. If you find yourself frustrated by message limits or hitting the cap before finishing a task, upgrade one tool — the one you use most. Most people only need one paid subscription.
sections.explanation: Paying for multiple AI tools you rarely use is wasted money. Try free tiers first, identify which one you reach for most, and upgrade that one if the limits become a real bottleneck.

sections.prompt: You're using a free AI plan and notice responses getting slower and less capable in the afternoon. What's the most likely explanation?

choices: The AI needs to be restarted
You've hit the free tier's daily usage limit and been switched to a less capable model
The AI is learning from your past messages and getting confused
data.explanation: Free plans have usage limits. When you hit them, many tools automatically switch you to an older or less capable model rather than cutting you off entirely. Upgrading to a paid plan removes these limits and keeps you on the best model all day.

sections.prompt: You're about to use a free AI tool to help write a sensitive internal business proposal. What should you check first?

choices: Whether the tool has a dark mode
Whether the free plan uses your conversations for model training
How many messages you get per day
Whether the tool has a mobile app
data.explanation: Many free AI tools may use your conversations to improve their models, which means humans could review them. For sensitive business content, check the privacy settings or upgrade to a paid plan that explicitly excludes training on your data.
